item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I love it too. +1
And it's going in my lexicon of wonderful nonsensical sentences just under 'All hail the glow cloud'.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea of someone being forced to speak in word salad is really cool

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Repetition at its finest. When you find a phrase as poetic as "A Great Big Nothing in the Middle of his Head", it's hard not to repeat it over and over, and you took that and rolled with it. Truly magical, how well done this SCP file is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is absolutely nothing about this article that I don't love. Infohazards are great, having to speak around something is always a fun style challenge, and I have a big soft spot for skips that have an infectious/consuming/expanding vibe.

item.title: Re: +100
item.content:encoded: goodwork puzzlemath
upvote greatbig

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Source: https://www.maxpixel.net/photo-971889
Licence: CC Public Domain

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Currently, you have an unsourced image within your article. Please source this image by replying to the sent PM and/or this post within 48 hours or they will have to be deleted. Also, make sure the image complies with the SCP Wiki's CC-BY-SA 3.0 license by following this handy guide, otherwise it will have to be deleted.

Timer has expired, the image has been removed. Please PM with the source in order to have it restored.

Image has been restored.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the idea is that his disordered thinking is keeping it contained by starving it. He tries to avoid forming thoughts/speech the same way as humans usually do because doing so feeds the Great Big Nothing.

Notably, his speech does have order to it - it has patterns and consistent terminology - but I think because that order is different from what the entity seems to feed on, it can't.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Except if he experiences natural brain death and dies, they would still perform an autopsy. As far as I can tell, the same risk is run with the path he has chosen. Therefore the better course of action would have been to go somewhere remote (where nobody would find the body until tGBNioHH rotted to the point where it was indistinguishable from the rest of his brain.) , and kill himself. Turning himself in to a hospital where an MRI could easily discover “The Great Big Nothing” just makes no sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Random thought: Something anomalous must be keeping the original great big thing in the middle of his head from killing him, which isn't present in child instances.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: DrKevlar in my opinion it's not that it spreads physically. It's that if he dies then people will perform an autopsy on him, and odds are that someone will, by accident, refer to it by anything other than the Great Big Nothing in the Middle of his Head, and than they're in trouble. Other than this little hiccup in the story, otherwise a genius concept and was well executed. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I also have this question. Want to +1 it, but novote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Something about the redacted part of his head, just over that utterly serene and innocent smile, combined with the "great big nothing in the middle of his head", creeped me out on an incredibly visceral level. In particular, his lips- there's just SOMETHING wrong with the position of those lips.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow! This guy seems like he mujrfyhgkkkkkkkkkkkkkkkkkkkkkjjjjjjj

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So wait, why does he have to experience natural brain death to destroy it? I’m genuinely confused. Can it spread through physical methods as well as verbal if he dies any other way? If not, why has he not commit suicide instead of playing the waiting game? I feel like there’s something I’m not understanding about the story, can somebody please explain?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This scared the absolute hell out of me. It took three tries to even finish the article. I somehow linked the great big nothing in the middle of his head to the black bar on the image, so that, too, became creepy.
Amazing skip, would get scared again, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seconding scpcrnp in that I feel as though the phrase is diluted a touch with the amount of repetition - I think even a hair less would be nice.

I was sold on this by the interviews, which do a decent job of shedding light on the situation in a roundabout way. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! It came to me in a dream and I had to write it. I thought it had a nice rhythm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I also enjoyed the phrase, though I think it was repeated one too many times and started to lose its effect.

Ultimately, this is like a nice diet mix of Taboo's nomenclative hazard and Bush v Gore's Garber-speak. I think there is a conceptual inconsistency here; that SCP-4022 can be understood implies a minimal degree of syntactical and logical structure, which seemingly could be "recognized" and consumed by…tGBNitMoHH.

But this is well written, well paced, and I feel that the angst you portrayed in trying to make as much sense with as little communicative structure as possible is a real horror to imagine, so +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love the great big nothing in the middle of his head. It's just so fucking musical. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I personally much prefer the Great Big Nothing In The Middle Of His Head

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's fun to read these SCPs with a very specific idea or object fully cut off and censored, and this one is no exception, but I think it would help if you changed the "Great big nothing in the middle of his head" to something more formal, or more sinister, like "There's a Nothing in its head" or a Void, to make it both more dramatic and scientific.
Apart from that, a nice entry to start of the non-contest Series V.
